paragraphs.text: Video games as a service have an expiry date, which means that games disappear once the service ends, despite having paid for the experience. In some video games, gacha is used as a form of unblocking without favouritism on the part of the player, a purchase of a major unit or resource can be carried out with the less chosen character, or old units and resources and not so important as to allow something similar to the system of gacha with that purchase. This can prevent favouritism such as the gacha system implemented with the aim of ensuring that for every purchase of unit or major resource it gives an important unit or resource that players would not want for favouritism. As an offer of 2x1. As it would be the equivalent of a way of acquiring important new units or resources, also the letter given free of charge because it is not a player’s favourite would have to be new. If the same unit or resource is required repetitively to make it stronger, bearing in mind that the gache would no longer exist, it would be unlocked by playing the video game or paying for it. Other unlocked currency is the Premium Currency or Tokens to obtain important units or resources, rather than directly paying it to Tokens in most cases. In theory, ‘Gratis de Jugar’ service games are ‘Gratis de Jugar’ but, in practice, ‘Gratis de Jugar’ service games are ‘Pagar por Ganar’, why the Gacha space is known, what is the most likely market, such as lottery, most of them lose, but you have the chance to earn something, only that bullet for the gacha lottery is more than EUR 1/Single Tirada, not that of 10, which they advise. There is not even the possibility of completing important units or resources in these ‘Gratis de Jugar’ service games without paying. And I am also aware of the practice of adding more rewards and distributing them separately for the entire game in order to slow down players and receive more income, even if the rewards were simplified, as the default players cannot even complete neither collections nor play as a service. Some gacha video games also have a shop of important units or resources that you can only receive for participating in distinctive forms of games in a limited time called Events. Major Event units or resources are added after three months. The Events are to make a pay-back to win the special resource of a type of Event that can only be obtained once, the Event usually lasts two weeks and once it ends, depending on the ‘participation’ and the payment or amount of Token/Moneda Premium Gratis who have received the letter and other copies of the unit (s) that make it stronger. An Event is a theoretical concertation, a different way of playing, therefore the reward makes sense, the problem is the ‘Paga por Beneficio’, which is ‘Paga para Ganar’. If you have to pay to earn or make a profit, it is not a video game, it is a market. This is also the recent practice of limiting important units or resources, in which case it is not even necessary to obtain the resource if its appearance is to be limited to a type of possibility in the gache, which means that it will not appear in another type of possibility in other gache, before at least possible to obtain all the important units or resources as it is not a limited major unit or resource. I am not even sure if they play the game to see that it is possible to unlock everything without paying. A video game is a complete product. A video game as a service is a game under development and therefore progressing. Paying for something under construction is not the best idea, especially when you will remove it once it ends and is a product. This is to pay for something you don’t have.
